data.content:
cause,economic factor,economic impact,investor concern	Market Selloff
Regulatory Uncertainty
The relation (Market Selloff, Regulatory Uncertainty) describes a causal connection wherein regulatory uncertainty acts as a primary catalyst for a market selloff. Specifically, regulatory uncertainty generates or heightens investor concerns, which in turn drives the subsequent market selloff. In this dynamic, the lack of regulatory clarity first unsettles investors, and these resulting concerns directly contribute to the broad selling of assets in the market.
